qa_pairs.question: Which record type carries a normalized parameter value set together with its per-case marks and optional id?
qa_pairs.answer: `ParameterSet` carries one normalized value set, any marks attached to that set, and an optional id.

qa_pairs.question: How does pytest make a fixture declared with parameters produce multiple collected test cases?
qa_pairs.answer: During fixture-driven generation, pytest finds a needed fixture definition with parameter values and calls metafunc parametrization indirectly with the fixture name, values, scope, and ids. Those planned calls later become separate collected function items.

qa_pairs.question: Why does a skip mark placed on one value in a parametrized fixture affect only that generated invocation?
qa_pairs.answer: The selected parameter set's marks are copied into the generated call spec, and when that call spec becomes a Function item its marks are added to that item's own markers. The skipping plugin then evaluates markers per item, so only the invocation built from that marked value is skipped.
